t_ch: …你怎么一脸嫌弃啊？
t_en: ...Why do you look so unimpressed?

t_ch: 你仔细想想，是以太复印欸！这套技术，我只在星核猎手那位小姑娘手里看到过……
t_en: Seriously, think about it, it's Aether copying! I've only ever heard of that Stellaron Hunter girl using this kind of tech.

t_ch: 走吧走吧，就当是陪我啦！
t_en: Come on, let's go. Just think of it as... keeping me company for a while!

t_ch: 铆钉镇的商业广场，对吧？事不宜迟，赶快出发~
t_en: It's at the Rivet Town Plaza, right? Let's hit the road, there's no time like the present!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#小姐}！我就知道你们一定会来！
t_en: {M#Mr. }{F#Miss. }{NICKNAME}! I knew you'd come!

t_ch: 来吧来吧，聊聊《以太战线》——我可是十八年老玩家了！你们有什么想要了解的，我一定知无不言！
t_en: Come on, let's chat about Aetherium Wars! I've been playing it for <unbreak>18</unbreak> years, so ask me anything — I'll tell you everything I know!

t_ch: 这到底是个什么游戏？
t_en: What kind of game is this, exactly?

t_ch: 这到底是个什么游戏呀？能麻烦你再给我们介绍一下嘛？
t_en: Yeah! Can you give us an overview?

t_ch: 不麻烦、不麻烦！只要是以太战线，我介绍多少遍都不嫌烦！
t_en: Why, it'd be my pleasure! I could talk to you about Aetherium Wars all day!

t_ch: 《以太战线》——看这名字您就明白了。这是一款基于「以太复印」技术，以「以太硬币」为载体的集换式卡牌对战游戏。
t_en: Aetherium Wars — the name says it all. It's a collectible card game that uses Aether copying tech, and Aether Coins are what you use to play.

t_ch: 通过这枚「以太硬币」，玩家可以将自己遇见的生物以「以太」的形式存储下来——还能将其实体化为「以太灵」与其他玩家「对决」。
t_en: Using an Aether Coin, players can store creatures they've encountered in the form of an Aether — and then manifest them as Aether Spirits to battle against other players.

t_ch: 「收集，对决，养成，升级」——这就是我们以太战线的宣传口号！
t_en: "Collect, Battle, Train, and Upgrade" — that's Aetherium Wars motto!

t_ch: 「以太复印」是什么？
t_en: What's "Aether copying?"

t_ch: 公司竟然也懂「以太复印」技术嘛？我还以为这是朋克洛德的骇客们才玩得转的东西呢。
t_en: I never expected the Corporation to know anything about Aether copying tech! I thought that was something only Punklordian hackers could handle.

t_ch: 哈哈，您说得对。这套技术本身发源自朋克洛德，但在公司和博识学会的不懈努力下，我们也初步了解了如何编辑以太。
t_en: Haha, you're right. The technology itself originated from Punklorde, but with the relentless efforts of the IPC and the Intelligentsia Guild, we've also gained a basic understanding of how to edit Aether.

t_ch: 不过，我们暂时还做不到像那群骇客一样随意篡改现实数据…但请相信我，这一天总会来的！
t_en: We might not be able to manipulate the data of reality as easily as those hackers can, but we're not far behind!

t_ch: 我看还是别来了吧…听起来怪危险的。
t_en: I think it'd be better if the IPC avoided it... it sounds pretty dangerous.

t_ch: 「星际决胜庆典」是什么？
t_en: What's the Interastral Tournament Festival?

t_ch: 你短信里说的那个「星际决胜庆典」——那是什么？
t_en: Mm, you mentioned something about the "Interastral Tournament Festival" — what is that?

t_ch: 好问题！这场庆典实际上是「以太战线竞技协会」在每赛季末举行的、面向全宇宙玩家的竞技赛事。
t_en: Great question! The festival is actually a tournament organized by the Aetherium Wars Gaming Association at the end of each season, and is open to players from across the universe.

t_ch: 宇宙各地的玩家将在这段时间内齐聚一堂，相互交流，共同享受游戏的快乐！
t_en: Gamers will come together to relax, share tips and tricks, and have a blast playing the game!

t_ch: 不过，由于星核阻塞星际通路的缘故，本次庆典临时改为了线上观赛模式——
t_en: However, due to Stellarons blocking interstellar routes, the festival was changed to online attendance at the last minute...

t_ch: ——尽管如此，对于那些执意想要到场观赛的观众，我们的线下会场依旧会向他们开放！
t_en: Nevertheless, for those who insist on attending in person, our offline venue is still open to them.

t_ch: …这协会办事还挺人性化的嘛。
t_en: Sounds like the association really cares about their players.

t_ch: 你从哪里拿到我的号码的？
t_en: How did you get my number?

t_ch: 呃…哈哈，这个……
t_en: Uh... Haha, well, that's...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，别难为人家啦。「公司」的名声会变成现在这样，也不是他一个人导致的。
t_en: Ugh, do we have to start an interrogation right now? I know the IPC aren't exactly our friends, but that's not just his fault.

t_ch: 我们想要体验这款游戏。
t_en: We want to try the game out.

t_ch: 对对对，我们很感兴趣——那，我们在哪里可以体验到这款游戏？
t_en: That's right! Any idea where we can give it a spin?

t_ch: 没想到……
t_en: I can't believe it...

t_ch: <size=36>我终于有成功把这游戏推销出去的一天了！</size>
t_en: <size=36>I finally managed to successfully promote the game!</size>

t_ch: 呃…有那么困难吗？难道是因为这游戏不好玩……
t_en: Uh... is it that difficult? Wait, does this game suck?

t_ch: 不不不，我只是…哎呀，身为老玩家的感动，你们恐怕很难理解吧！
t_en: No, no, of course not. It's just... as someone who's been playing this game for so long... I guess it's hard for those who haven't played it to understand.

t_ch: 我以自己的名誉做担保，这是全宇宙最尖端的娱乐形式！不好玩，你们找我！
t_en: I swear on my reputation that this game is truly the best form of entertainment out there! I'll take the rap if you don't like it!

t_ch: 事不宜迟，既然二位有兴趣，我就带着二位前往庆典服务大厅注册账号吧。跟我来，这边请！
t_en: There's no time to waste! Since you're both interested, let me take you to the Festival Service Hall to register your accounts. This way!

t_ch: 二位，请看！这里就是本届「星际决胜庆典」的舞台。
t_en: Take a look, this is where the Interastral Tournament Festival will be held this year.

t_ch: 为了让重新焕发生机的雅利洛-Ⅵ更快赶上流行趋势，竞技协会特意选择了这颗星球……
t_en: The Aetherium Wars Gaming Association specifically chose this planet to help rejuvenate it and bring it up to date with the latest trends...

t_ch: 不错，不错…一切都在按计划执行。
t_en: Good, good... Everything is going according to plan.

t_ch: 咦？那不是……
t_en: Is that...?

t_ch: 乔瓦尼先生！是您吗？
t_en: Mr. Giovanni, is that you?

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Hmm?

t_ch: 哈，维尔德！我正在想你去哪儿了呢——
t_en: Ha, Wilder! I was just wondering where you'd got to...

t_ch: 竟然还带来了{M#一位英俊的先生和这位美丽的小姐}{F#两位美丽的小姐}！欢迎光临我的竞技场！
t_en: ...and you brought along {M#a handsome gentleman and a beautiful young lady}{F#two beautiful young ladies}! Welcome to my arena!

t_ch: 如果我没猜错，两位就是{NICKNAME}和三月七，对吗？
t_en: If I'm not mistaken, you're the trailblazers, right?

t_ch: 难道两位也是听说了「星际决胜庆典」，想要前来一展身手么？
t_en: So, I presume you two have heard about the Interastral Tournament Festival? Have you come to show off your skills?

t_ch: 喂，{NICKNAME}…你看他的面具，挺酷的呀？
t_en: Psst, {NICKNAME} look at his mask! Pretty cool, don't you think?

t_ch: 猜得不错，在哪儿报名？
t_en: Correct! Where do we sign up?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，果然如我所料——你和传闻中描述的一模一样！
t_en: Hahaha, just as I expected — you're exactly as they say in the rumors!

t_ch: 你怎么知道我们的名字？
t_en: How did you know who we are?

t_ch: 这不重要，朋友。重要的是我们在这里相遇了！
t_en: It doesn't matter, my friend. What matters is that we're now acquainted!

t_ch: 面具怪人——还不快快报上名来！
t_en: Yo, mask-face, introduce yourself.

t_ch: 哈哈，你看我——真是失态！
t_en: Haha, look at me, forgetting my manners.

t_ch: 忘记自我介绍了——鄙人名叫乔瓦尼•迪•乔尔吉奥•达•艾普瑟隆——来自艾普瑟隆的小小私商，本届「星际决胜庆典」的全权赞助人……
t_en: My name is Giovanni di Giorgio da Epsilon, a humble private trader from Epsilon, and the sole sponsor of this year's Interastral Tournament Festival...

t_ch: …还请二位多多关照。
t_en: ...If there's anything I can do for you, don't hesitate to ask.

t_ch: 感谢您的自我介绍，乔瓦尼•迪•乔…什么什么先生。
t_en: Thanks for introducing yourself, Mr. Giovanni Di... something something...

t_ch: 您听起来是个厉害的大人物…怎么会出现在这里呢？
t_en: You seem like a pretty important person. What brings you to this kind of place?

t_ch: 太失礼了，三月。
t_en: That's not very polite, March.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，无伤大雅、无伤大雅！我反而很喜欢三月小姐这直率的性格。
t_en: Hahaha, no harm done! Your candid personality is charming, Miss March.

t_ch: 是啊，为什么呢？
t_en: Yeah! What brings you here?

t_ch: 不愧是拯救了贝洛伯格的开拓者！您果然如此敏锐，不同凡响！
t_en: You truly live up to your reputation as the saviors of Belobog. Such keen insight!

t_ch: 能把面具摘了让我看看不？
t_en: Can you take off your mask, so I can get a better look at you?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，您果然很幽默！但很可惜，这副面具事关我的生命安全——就让我们为彼此留些私人空间，好吗？
t_en: Hahaha, how humorous! As much as I'd like to oblige, this mask is a necessary precaution for me. Let's leave something to the imagination, shall we?

t_ch: 接下来，就让我回答一下三月小姐的问题吧——如您所见，「星际决胜庆典」即将在这颗美丽的冰雪星球举办……
t_en: Now, let me answer Miss March's question — as you can see, the Interastral Tournament Festival is about to be held on this beautiful icy planet...

t_ch: 而我作为本次赛事的全权赞助人，前来大赛会场检查运营状况，也是顺理成章。
t_en: ...and as the sole sponsor of this event, it's only natural for me to come to the tournament venue and check on its operations.

t_ch: 再者，我也想认识认识大赛的玩家，还有二位——在这茫茫宇宙里做生意，朋友总不嫌多。
t_en: Furthermore, I'd also like to get to know the players of the tournament, including yourselves. One can never have too many friends when it comes to doing business in this vast universe of ours.

t_ch: 如果二位还愿意赏光叫我一声朋友，那可真是我莫大的荣幸了！
t_en: If you'd be willing to call me "friend," it would be a great honor to have you as such!

t_ch: 唔…这家伙虽然戴着面具，一副怪里怪气的样子，还是个大人物……
t_en: Hmm... weird mask, strange vibes, a little out of touch...

t_ch: 但他好像没有恶意，还挺平易近人的。你怎么看，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: ...but he seems friendly enough, right, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 为我们的友谊，干杯！
t_en: A toast to our new friendship!

t_ch: 嗯，我也相信你的判断。那就请多指教啦，乔瓦尼先生。
t_en: I suppose... Pleasure to meet you, Mr. Giovanni.

t_ch: 哈哈，能交到你们这样的朋友，今天真是我人生中最快乐的日子了。
t_en: Haha, to have made friends like you — what a joyous day.

t_ch: 无事献殷勤，非奸即盗！
t_en: Something's off...

t_ch: 嗯，我也有点担心这个…但我确实感受不到他的恶意欸。
t_en: Yeah... but he really doesn't seem like a bad guy.

t_ch: 哈哈，我理解！既然如此，那就让我再多展示一些诚意吧。
t_en: Haha, I understand your sentiment! In that case, allow me to show you a little more sincerity.

t_ch: 那还请务必按月付我朋友费…
t_en: Monthly friendship payments are due on the first of each month...

t_ch: …原来你在意的是这个啊？！
t_en: Is that all you care about!?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，{NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#小姐}竟比我还会开玩笑！我就当这是您答应鄙人的不情之请了，如何？
t_en: Hahahaha, that's a good one, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}! You're even funnier than I imagined! I'll take that as an acceptance of our friendship!

t_ch: 那就请多指教啦，乔瓦尼先生。
t_en: Pleasure to meet you, Mr. Giovanni.

t_ch: 关于《以太战线》这款游戏，想必维尔德已经为你们介绍清楚了。那么，有关「星际决胜庆典」的部分，就让我来解答你们的疑惑吧！
t_en: I believe Wilder has already filled you in on Aetherium Wars, but if you have any questions about the Interastral Tournament Festival, I'll be more than happy to answer them for you!

t_ch: 乔瓦尼先生，这是我分内的工作，还请让我来……
t_en: Mr. Giovanni, let me take care of it, it's my job, after all...

t_ch: 没关系，维尔德！难得交到了朋友，不让我来介绍怎么能行呢——你先去忙其他事吧，接下来由我为两位做向导就好。
t_en: Don't worry, Wilder! It's only natural that I introduce the festival to my new friends here. You can go ahead and attend to other matters while I show these two around.

t_ch: 多谢乔瓦尼先生！我先告辞了。
t_en: Thank you, Mr. Giovanni! I'll be off, then.

t_ch: 请两位尽管向我提问吧——有关大赛会场和「星际决胜庆典」，你们有什么想要了解的内容吗？
t_en: Feel free to ask me about the Interastral Tournament Festival and the venue. Is there anything in particular you'd like to know?

t_ch: 这是个什么地方？
t_en: What is this place?

t_ch: 这里？是贝洛伯格的武器试验场——至少七百年之前是。
t_en: Belobog's weapon testing ground — at least it was <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago.

t_ch: 在修复这里的过程中，我们发现了许多条尘封的自动机兵流水线。几百年前，贝洛伯格人就是在这片场地里测试机兵性能的。
t_en: While renovating this place, we stumbled upon quite a few automaton production lines, all covered in thick layers of dust. Several hundred years ago, the people of Belobog tested the performance of their combat automatons here.

t_ch: 至于现在，在我的赞助下，公司和以太战线竞技协会将这里改造成了大赛的比赛场地——换句话说，这现在是属于我的「竞技场」了。
t_en: But now, under my sponsorship, the IPC and the Aetherium Wars Gaming Association have transformed this place into the competition venue for the tournament — in other words, this place is now my "arena."

t_ch: 原来如此。
t_en: I see.

t_ch: 你这工程合规吗？
t_en: Is this project compliant with regulations?

t_ch: 当然合规！这项工程可是得到贝洛伯格建筑行会会长和文化大臣亲自批准的——
t_en: Of course! This project has been approved personally by the president of the Belobog Architecture Guild and the Minister of Culture.

t_ch: 至于他们是否得到了那位大守护者的首肯…这我就不方便过问了。
t_en: As for whether they received the approval of the Supreme Guardian, well, that's beyond my knowledge and involvement.

t_ch: 以上就是我所能提供的全部信息了。你还有任何疑问吗？
t_en: That's all I can share about that at the moment. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

t_ch: 庆典就在这里举办吗？
t_en: Is the festival going to be held here?

t_ch: 当然！看到底下的巨大圆盘了吗？等到庆典锦标赛正式开幕时，这里就会成为以太战线玩家们证明自己实力的舞台了。
t_en: Indeed! Have you seen the big circular platform down below? When the championship commences, this space will serve as the arena for Aetherium Wars players to showcase their skills, and where they'll vie for supremacy.

t_ch: 在庆典举行期间，你可以在这里与来自宇宙各地的玩家交流心得，养成自己的「以太灵」，相互切磋——
t_en: During the festival, you'll have the chance to mingle with players from all over the universe, share experiences, level up your Aether Spirits, and challenge each other.

t_ch: ——哦，抱歉，我的失误。本次赛事由于众所周知的星核问题，许多玩家将无法实地到场…所以，你恐怕只能见到些熟悉面孔了。
t_en: Ah yes, my apologies. I should have mentioned this earlier, but because of the well-known Stellaron problem, many players won't be able to attend in person... So, it's likely you'll mostly encounter familiar faces during the event.

t_ch: 我该如何参加庆典？
t_en: How do I participate?

t_ch: 很简单！首先，你们需要在这个庆典服务大厅的终端上注册你的账号——据我所知，你们还没有属于自己的游戏账号，对吧？
t_en: It's simple! First, you need to register your account at a terminal in this Festival Service Hall. Oh, you haven't created one, correct?

t_ch: 在那之后，我会向各位仔细介绍本届庆典的内容…至于现在，就让我们留个悬念吧。
t_en: After you've successfully created and registered your account, I'll delve into all the details of the festival... But for now, you'll have to deal with the suspense for just a little bit longer.

t_ch: 总觉得背后有什么阴谋…
t_en: Why do I feel like you're up to something?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，太过疑神疑鬼对身体可不好。
t_en: Hahaha, you've certainly got quite the active imagination!

t_ch: 放心，我只不过是热衷于享受游戏的快乐，更想看看玩家们为此相互竞争、携手共进的样子——
t_en: Rest assured, I'm just passionate about gaming. I love observing how players compete and cooperate with each other.

t_ch: 除此之外，我没有其他任何想法了：我可以向你保证——以我的名誉和这副面具起誓！
t_en: I have no hidden agendas, I can assure you of that — I swear on my reputation and my mask!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: No further questions.

t_ch: 哎，我有我有——我想提问！
t_en: Actually... I have a question.

t_ch: 说了这么多，可以先让我们实际体验一下这个游戏嘛？只是听你嘴上介绍，我还是有点摸不着头脑欸。
t_en: You've been explaining a lot about the venue, but I'm still confused about the game. Can we actually give it a go?

t_ch: 当然没问题！这样，我会破例把自己的以太灵借给你，让你好好体验一把「对决」的快乐……
t_en: Why of course, not a problem! I'll make an exception just for you. You can borrow my Aether Spirits and experience the thrill of battle...

t_ch: …嗯？那里怎么闹哄哄的…发生什么事了？
t_en: ...Hmm? What's all the commotion?

t_ch: 喂！臭小子！把你手里那硬币交出来！
t_en: Hey, twerp! Hand over that coin!

t_ch: 你这小屁孩，从小手脚就不干净，长大后还得了？
t_en: You little brat — who said you could touch this?

t_ch: 这…这是我捡来的……
t_en: B—But this is mine, I found it on the ground...

t_ch: 哎，你们几个，差不多得了！欺负小孩算什么本事？
t_en: Hey, that's enough! Picking on a kid, are we?

t_ch: 外来者姐姐！他们、他们好像很危险……
t_en: O—Outworlder lady! Careful, they're dangerous...

t_ch: 没事，这里交给我们就好。
t_en: Don't sweat it — leave them to us.

t_ch: 我说啊，几位公司的客人们——看你们对那枚硬币紧咬不放的样子，想必你们也是《以太战线》的玩家吧？
t_en: I say, my IPC friends, I can see how captivated you are by that coin. Am I right in assuming that you're also Aetherium Wars players?

t_ch: 哪儿来的面具男？我是不是玩家关你什么事？
t_en: Where did mask-dude come from? And what does it have to do with you whether I'm a player or not?

t_ch: 就是就是！关你什么事？戴着面具鬼鬼祟祟，你怕不是个变态吧？
t_en: Exactly! What's it to you? Sneaking around with a mask on — you must be some kind of creep.

t_ch: 呵呵，我是说——既然各位都是玩家，何不按照以太战线的规则进行一场「对决」呢？
t_en: Hehe, what I'm getting at is this — since you're both players, why not have a showdown to settle the matter? Following the proper rules of the game, of course.

t_ch: 我提议，让{M#这位先生和三月小姐}{F#这两位小姐}代替这位小朋友向你们发起挑战——
t_en: But, instead of this young boy, I propose that {M#this gentleman and Miss March}{F#these two young ladies} be the challengers.

t_ch: 如果{M#他们}{F#她们}输了，这枚硬币自然是各位的战利品，任各位随意处置……
t_en: If they lose, the coin will naturally become yours, and you can do as you wish with it...

t_ch: 但如果你们输了，就只好麻烦你们收起獠牙，至少别在我的竞技场里惹是生非——不知各位意下如何？
t_en: ...but if you lose, I would kindly ask that you gracefully accept defeat and refrain from causing any more trouble in my arena. Are we all in agreement?

t_ch: 这主意不错！
t_en: Sounds like a great idea!

t_ch: 嘿，我就知道你会这么选！
t_en: Hah! I had a feeling you'd say that!

t_ch: 我没玩过这游戏啊…
t_en: But I've never played this game before...

t_ch: 没关系，乔瓦尼先生肯定会指导我们的——对吧？
t_en: Not to worry! Mr. Giovanni will explain it all, right?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，那是自然！我一定不遗余力，协助二位旗开得胜。
t_en: Hahaha, why of course! I assure you that I will do everything in my power to assist you both.

t_ch: 我能用球棍解决问题吗？
t_en: How about I use a bat to resolve our differences?

t_ch: 我不建议这么做——要是用暴力解决问题可太没意思了。
t_en: Ahem, I wouldn't advise it. Violence wouldn't make for a very interesting outcome now, would it?

t_ch: 答应我，不要做无趣的人，好吗？
t_en: Let's all agree to keep things peaceful and enjoyable.

t_ch: 乔瓦尼先生说得对！正好，我们也借这个机会，体验体验这款游戏吧。
t_en: Mr. Giovanni is right! Now's our chance to check out this game and see if it lives up to the hype.

t_ch: 给，{NICKNAME}，这是几只最初级的以太灵——你可以利用它们来熟悉节奏规则。
t_en: Here are a few beginner-level Aether Spirits for you, {NICKNAME}. They'll help you get the hang of the game's pace and rules.

t_ch: 再者，如果二位客人连这种等级的以太灵都解决不了…那你们可能确实不太适合这款游戏。
t_en: If you have trouble handling these beginner spirits... it might indicate that this game isn't the best choice for you.

t_ch: 该、该死，竟敢看不起我们——
t_en: Ha! You've got some nerve...

t_ch: 我们一定要让你们尝尝「托帕后援团」成员的厉害！
t_en: You're in for a rude awakening — prepare to face the might of "Topaz's Support Squad!"

t_ch: 好啦，那群坏蛋都被我们赶跑了！你没事吧，埃里克？
t_en: We sure showed those troublemakers who's boss! Are you okay, Eric?

t_ch: 我、我没事…谢谢外来者姐姐！
t_en: Thanks, Miss March, I—I'm fine.

t_ch: 人没事就好。
t_en: Glad to hear it.

t_ch: 你没事就好。所以，公司的员工为什么来威胁你呢？
t_en: Good. Why were those IPC workers threatening you?

t_ch: 他们为什么威胁你？
t_en: Why were they threatening you?

t_ch: 是啊，我也想问——他们怎么会来威胁一个小孩子？
t_en: I was wondering the same thing. Why would they pick on someone half their size?

t_ch: 这游戏也太简单了吧？
t_en: This game is way too easy.

t_ch: 哎呀，不愧是你——不过胜利感言还是等下再说吧。
t_en: Here we go.... Let's save the victory speeches for later.

t_ch: 所以，埃里克，他们怎么会来威胁你呢？发生什么事了？
t_en: So, Eric, why did they threaten you like that. What happened?

t_ch: 我、我也不知道…我只是偶然在路上捡到了这枚硬币……
t_en: I—I don't know... I just found this coin on the floor...

t_ch: 嗯…单看硬币外表是看不出什么名堂，也不知道是谁遗失的。
t_en: Hmm... based on the appearance of the coin alone, it's difficult to discern any significance. As for who it belongs to — that's also a mystery.

t_ch: 他们还说什么了吗——比如，和「托帕」相关的事？
t_en: Didn't they mention something else? "Topaz"?

t_ch: 对啊，他们提到了托帕小姐的名字！到底是怎么一回事？
t_en: Yeah, they mentioned Miss Topaz! What's that all about?

t_ch: 我想起来了！他们说过，自己是那位「托帕」的后援团成员…还说要把全宇宙最强力的以太灵全都搜刮过来，送给「托帕」…这样她就能在庆典上连冠了！

t_ch: 啊？！托帕小姐她…居然这么不择手段？

t_ch: 我想大概是某种误会。我和托帕小姐有些交集，这不是她的待人接物之道。

t_ch: 我没有任何意见——「交换」、「养成」，这都是《以太战线》游戏的一环。你们就好好利用这枚硬币吧。

t_ch: 那，就交给{M#哥哥姐姐}{F#姐姐们}了！我先回家了，老爹还在家里等着我呢。

t_ch: 祝各位享受这场庆典——我们下一站再见，{M#英俊的先生和美丽的小姐}{F#两位美丽的小姐}！
t_en: I wish you all a memorable festival! Farewell for now, dear friends.

t_ch: 好啦，该学的东西都学会了…是时候开始我们的旅途了！
t_en: Okay, looks like we've learned all the essentials — let the fun begin!

t_ch: 出发，前往第一个「决胜乐园」——<color=#dbc291ff>残响回廊</color>！
t_en: Let's head towards the first Victory Zone — <color=#dbc291ff>Corridor of Fading Echoes</color>!

t_ch: 那身制服…是给你发消息的公司员工吗？
t_en: That uniform... Was it him that texted you?

t_ch: 这里…居然还有这么大的空间吗？
t_en: ...Such a huge area.

t_ch: 您说得不错——我们公司看中了雅利洛-Ⅵ的潜力，特地将这座旧武器试验场给翻新了。
t_en: Mm-hmm — the Corporation saw the potential in Jarilo-VI and decided to renovate this old weapon testing ground.

t_ch: 当然，「武器试验场」已经是过去式了——现在它是「星际决胜庆典」的大赛会场。
t_en: Haha, don't worry, you won't find any "weapon testing" going on here. Now it's the venue for the Interastral Tournament Festival!

t_ch: 咦？这里的氛围感觉有些不一样…好热闹呀。
t_en: The atmosphere here is so different now... It's so lively!

t_ch: 哈哈，原来二位已经来过这里了？最近，公司看中了雅利洛-Ⅵ的潜力，特地将这座旧武器试验场给翻新了。
t_en: Haha, so you guys have been here before? The Corporation recently saw the potential in Jarilo-VI and decided to renovate this old weapon testing ground.

t_ch: 当然，「武器试验场」是过去式了——现在它是「星际决胜庆典」的大赛会场。
t_en: Haha, don't worry, you won't find any "weapon testing" going on here. Now it's the venue for the Interastral Tournament Festival!

t_ch: 就是这儿啦，<color=#dbc291ff>「残响回廊的决胜乐园」</color>入口！
t_en: This is it! The entrance to the <color=#dbc291ff>Corridor of Fading Echoes Victory Zone</color>!

t_ch: 我刚刚又上星际互联网络查了查攻略，才知道这「决胜乐园」是公司和竞技协会专门向各个星球租借的场地——公司的腕儿可真大。
t_en: I just looked up some tips on the Interastral Network — these Victory Zones are leased by the IPC and the Gaming Association from various planets. The IPC's reach is impressive...

t_ch: 你准备好了吗，{NICKNAME}？我们出发吧～
t_en: Ready to head out, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 哼哼，哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink, oink!

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 决胜乐园里都会有些什么呢～真期待呀。
t_en: Can't wait to find out what's inside these Victory Zones!

t_ch: 嘿，乔瓦尼先生！我们又见面啦。
t_en: Mr. Giovanni, I didn't expect to see you again so soon.

t_ch: 哼哼？哼哼，哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink, oink! Oink!

t_ch: 哈哈，两位的动作很快嘛！欢迎来到<color=#dbc291ff>「残响回廊的决胜乐园」</color>！
t_en: Haha, you two move fast. Welcome to the <color=#dbc291ff>Corridor of Fading Echoes Victory Zone</color>!

t_ch: 你在这里做什么呢？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 好问题！作为赞助人，我正在视察每个游戏设施——先前的决胜庆典竞技场就是我的第一站。
t_en: Great question! As the event's sponsor, I'm tasked with inspecting all facilities. And this place — the previous tournament's arena — is my first port of call.

t_ch: 赞助人还得亲自下场玩游戏啊？
t_en: So, do sponsors have to play the game, too?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，不瞒你说，我本人也确实是这以太战线的资深玩家。
t_en: Hahaha, believe it or not, I'm actually somewhat of a seasoned Aetherium Wars player myself.

t_ch: 话说回来，两位恐怕是第一次进入<color=#dbc291ff>「决胜乐园」</color>吧？
t_en: Am I correct in saying that this is your first time entering a <color=#dbc291ff>Victory Zone</color>?

t_ch: 还记得我先前介绍的吗？玩家在决胜乐园中进行<color=#dbc291ff>收集游离数据</color>、<color=#dbc291ff>和其他玩家切磋</color>等种种活动，都可以提升自己的<color=#dbc291ff>「决胜者评级」</color>。
t_en: In case you want to hear the rules again — players can enhance their <color=#dbc291ff>Victory Rating</color> by engaging in various activities within the Victory Zone, such as <color=#dbc291ff>collecting roaming data</color> and <color=#dbc291ff>participating in sparring matches with other players</color>.

t_ch: 其中，<color=#dbc291ff>收集游离数据</color>更是相当重要的环节——因为这是玩家除了购买和交换外，唯一能够入手新以太灵的方法。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Collecting roaming data</color> is a vital aspect, because it's the only way for players to encounter new Aether Spirits, aside from purchasing or trading them.

t_ch: 正巧，两位也都在这里——不如就让我来教两位如何收集游离数据吧。
t_en: Since you're both here, let me give you a lesson on gathering roaming data.

t_ch: 咦，真的可以吗？会不会太麻烦您了……
t_en: Really? We know you're pretty busy...

t_ch: 没有的事——让各位新手玩家快速掌握游玩方法也是庆典的一环嘛。
t_en: It'd be my pleasure! Assisting new players in swiftly understanding the gameplay is just another part of the festival!

t_ch: 我就将这名为<color=#dbc291ff>外宇宙之炎</color>的以太数据赠给两位好了——有了它，你们收集游离数据想必会轻松不少。
t_en: I will gift you this Aether data — "<color=#dbc291ff>Blaze Out of Space</color>." With this, collecting roaming data should be much easier for the both of you.

t_ch: 这附近刚好有不少落单的游离数据。不如就拿它们来练手吧？
t_en: There happens to be a good number of roaming data targets in the vicinity. How about using them as practice?

t_ch: 哼哼，哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink, oink! Oink!

t_ch: 哦？看来你们还没有把队伍填满呢……
t_en: Hmm, it looks like your team isn't complete yet...

t_ch: 我强烈建议你们先把队伍填满再继续前进——凭你们现在的状态，恐怕很难对付后面的挑战者和冠军候补哦。
t_en: I strongly suggest you bolster your team before moving forward. Dealing with the upcoming challengers and championship contenders could be tough without a full roster.

t_ch: （收集更多游离数据，把队伍填满吧……）
t_en: (Better gather more roaming data and strengthen my team...)

t_ch: 嗯…不愧是大名鼎鼎的开拓者，只是这样简单体验过后就能上手——你们可真是天生的玩家。
t_en: Impressive! I see you're both living up to your renowned Trailblazer reputations! It would seem you two have a natural talent for gaming.

t_ch: 嘿嘿～不愧是我们俩！
t_en: Hehe~ That's us all right!

t_ch: 哼哼！哼～哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink, oink! Oink!

t_ch: 都是乔瓦尼先生教得好。
t_en: It's all thanks to your brilliant teaching, Mr. Giovanni.

t_ch: 嘿，还是你会说话。也确实，多谢乔瓦尼先生啦。
t_en: Yikes, sweet talk alert... But it's true, thank you, Mr. Giovanni!

t_ch: 不愧是三月七！
t_en: March never fails to impress!

t_ch: 哼哼，会夸你就多夸两句～
t_en: A compliment!? Continue...

t_ch: 不愧是我！
t_en: I never fail to impress!

t_ch: …哎呀，我就只是客套两句啦。真不愧是你。
t_en: Taking every chance to pat yourself on the back, I see.

t_ch: 嗯…让我看看，接下来应该是……
t_en: Let me see, what next...

t_ch: 哈，对了！这是我为各位准备的几枚<color=#dbc291ff>「以太拓展芯片」</color>——
t_en: Ah, that's it, now I remember! I prepared a few "<color=#dbc291ff>Aether Expansion Chips</color>" for you both.

t_ch: 只要用以太硬币读取这些芯片，你们就可以为以太灵附装各种各样的技能了——事不宜迟，我们现在就试试看吧。
t_en: You can use your Aether Coin to read the data on these chips. They allow your Aether Spirits to equip various abilities. Why not give them a go right now?

t_ch: 哟，大家都在啊——还有{NICKNAME}和小三月呢！是什么风把两位大英雄给吹来了啊～
t_en: Hey, everyone's here — {NICKNAME} and little March, too! What lucky charm lured these two great heroes our way?

t_ch: 哇，是希露瓦，还有佩拉！好久不见啦。
t_en: Serval, Pela! Has it really been that long!?

t_ch: 好久不见。
t_en: It's great to see you, too!

t_ch: 哈哈，两位<color=#dbc291ff>「冠军候补」</color>也登场了！这场戏可真是越来越热闹了啊。
t_en: Behold! Two <color=#dbc291ff>championship contenders</color>! It would seem the stage is set for some intense competition!

t_ch: 什么，希露瓦你…居然是这个决胜乐园的冠军候补吗？
t_en: Huh...!? S—Serval's the championship contender for this Victory Zone?

t_ch: 嘿，这下可要好好做些准备了。
t_en: Huh, I guess it's time we got our act together.

t_ch: 好久不见，各位！
t_en: Long time no see, everyone!

t_ch: 好久不见，外来者——还有希露瓦和…情报官大人！
t_en: Good to see you outsider, S—Serval, Pel— I—I mean, Intelligence Officer!

t_ch: 你们都在这儿埋伏我呢？
t_en: Is this an ambush?

t_ch: 好久不见了，外来者——这次我可不会再输给你了！
t_en: It's been a while, outsider. You won't get away with it this time!

t_ch: 咦，这游戏在贝洛伯格已经这么流行了吗？
t_en: Huh, so is this game already popular in Belobog?

t_ch: 是啊，毕竟在公司的人来之前，贝洛伯格的大家都不知道什么是电子游戏……
t_en: Yeah, but I guess no one in Belobog had much of a clue about video games before the IPC showed up...

t_ch: 不得不说，这个什么《以太战线》可真好玩——我都连着肝了好几天了，饭都没顾得上吃！
t_en: I've gotta say, Aetherium Wars is really fun, I've been grinding for several days straight — barely even had time to eat!

t_ch: 希望你干别的也有这个劲头……
t_en: I wish you'd put the same effort into other things...

t_ch: 到目前为止的感觉如何，希露瓦小姐和佩拉小姐？这<color=#dbc291ff>「冠军候补」</color>的身份还习惯吗？
t_en: So, Miss Serval, Miss Pela, how are you feeling? Have you gotten accustomed to the title of "<color=#dbc291ff>championship contender</color>" yet?

t_ch: 习惯了，习惯了～我和佩拉已经劝退不少新手玩家了。
t_en: Totally used to it~ Pela and I have already struck fear into the hearts of more than a few newbies.

t_ch: 这不是什么值得炫耀的事吧……
t_en: Should we really be bragging about something like this...

t_ch: 哈哈，习惯了就好！这座决胜乐园交给二位负责真是正确的决定。
t_en: Haha, it's good that you've gotten used to it! Entrusting the Victory Zone to the two of you was the right decision.

t_ch: 既然各位都在这里了——希露瓦小姐和佩拉小姐，不如就由两位冠军候补来向挑战者们介绍一下这里的规则吧？
t_en: Since you're all here, how about we let Miss Serval and Miss Pela, our championship contenders, explain the rules to everyone?

t_ch: 没问题！规则其实很简单——首先，我对铁卫的<color=#dbc291ff>照雪灯炉</color>进行了小小的改装，让它们可以相互传递热量……
t_en: No problem! The rules are actually quite simple. To start with, I've made some minor tweaks to the <color=#dbc291ff>Snowshine Lamps</color>, making it possible for heat to be transferred between them...

t_ch: 只要你把<color=#dbc291ff>热能核心</color>装填进一座灯炉，就可以同时点亮附近的所有灯炉了——怎么样，厉害吧？
t_en: Just pop a <color=#dbc291ff>thermic core</color> into a lamp, and all the nearby lamps will light up simultaneously — cool, right?

t_ch: 你们要做的就是用<color=#dbc291ff>数量有限的热能核心</color>点亮决胜乐园中<color=#dbc291ff>所有指定的照雪灯炉</color>。
t_en: The puzzle for you to solve is to use the <color=#dbc291ff>limited number of thermic cores</color> at your disposal to illuminate <color=#dbc291ff>all of the designated Snowshine Lamps</color> scattered around the Victory Zone.

t_ch: 百闻不如一见——你们就用这两座灯炉练练手吧！
t_en: Experience is always the best teacher — so go ahead and try for yourselves with these two lamps!

t_ch: 看，有了希露瓦改装的热能传递功能，你们就可以点亮那些无法抵达的灯炉了。
t_en: You see? Serval's modifications to the lamps allow you to illuminate lamps that are out of your reach.

t_ch: 但我们可不会轻易将锦标赛的资格徽章交给你们——所以，在挑战我们之前，一定记得做好充分准备。
t_en: But don't think we'll just hand over the Mastery Mark without a fight — you'd better make sure you're ready before you even think about challenging us!

t_ch: 欸～居然还专门设计了这种规则…难道每个决胜乐园都会有类似的设计吗？
t_en: Hmm~ After seeing this special modification... I can't help but wonder, do all Victory Zones have a similar kind of setup?

t_ch: 当然！毕竟这也是{RUBY_B#欢愉}庆典{RUBY_E#}的一部分——每座决胜乐园的谜题都有所不同，各位就尽情享受这场盛会吧！
t_en: Of course! After all, it's just another part of the {RUBY_B#The Elation}tournament{RUBY_E#}. Each Victory Zone has its own unique puzzles. But don't worry yourselves too much — the most important thing is that you have fun!

t_ch: 哈，感觉已经热血沸腾起来了…我一定会把资格徽章赢到手的！
t_en: Hah, I can feel the adrenaline rushing through my veins... That Mastery Mark is mine!

t_ch: 这次，我一定要向你证明我的摇滚之魂千真万确…对不住了，希露瓦！
t_en: B—Brace yourself, Serval! I'm about to rock your world!

t_ch: 大家看起来都干劲十足啊…{NICKNAME}，我们要不也说点什么？
t_en: Yikes, everyone's so fired up... {NICKNAME}, quick, let's say something...

t_ch: 目标是——以太战线大师！
t_en: Behold! You're looking at the soon-to-be Aetherium Wars grand champs!

t_ch: 好耶！我们的目标是——以太战线大师！
t_en: Yeah! Grand champs in the house!

t_ch: 等等，这个台词…咱俩不会出事吧？
t_en: Uh... I hope we haven't just jinxed ourselves.

t_ch: 让我们把决胜乐园烧成灰！
t_en: Why so SERIOUS!? Let's put a happy little fire on this place!

t_ch: 好耶！让我们把决胜乐园……
t_en: Woo! Yeah! Let's...

t_ch: 等会儿等会儿！这都哪儿跟哪儿啊，你被反物质军团的虚卒附体了是吧？
t_en: Geez, did you join the Antimatter Legion when I wasn't looking?

t_ch: 哼哼——哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink, oink!

t_ch: 哈哈，劲头不错。虽然我们也还没完全摸透这个游戏——但我和佩拉一定不会输给你们的！
t_en: Haha, the enthusiasm is cute, but don't think just because you're still figuring out the game that Pela and I will take it easy on you!

t_ch: 另外，这是<color=#dbc291ff>三枚热能核心</color>——就用它们解决决胜乐园的谜题，向我们证明你的实力吧。
t_en: There are <color=#dbc291ff>three thermic cores</color> — you'll need them to solve the puzzles in the Victory Zone. Let's see what you've got!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这才是庆典应该有的样子！投资这场盛会真是个正确的决定。
t_en: Hahaha, now that's exactly the festive atmosphere I'm looking for! Investing in this event was most certainly the right decision!

t_ch: 那么，我就先前往下一个决胜乐园视察了。祝两位旗开得胜！
t_en: Please excuse me, I must inspect the next Victory Zone. Best of luck to you both!

t_ch: 谢谢你，乔瓦尼先生。
t_en: Thank you, Mr. Giovanni.

t_ch: 我们走，{NICKNAME}——去挑战希露瓦和佩拉吧！
t_en: Let's get moving, {NICKNAME}! Time to show Pela and Serval what we're made of!

t_ch: 哼哼！哼——！
t_en: Oink! Oink, oink!

t_ch: 决胜乐园里还有几座类似的灯炉，你们只要把它们全部点亮，就可以顺利来到我和希露瓦面前，向我们发起挑战。
t_en: You'll find several lamps like this around the Victory Zone. Once you've illuminated all of them, seek out me and Serval. We'll be ready to throw down the gauntlet!

t_ch: 哟，{NICKNAME}和小三月！没想到第一个来到我们两人面前的居然是你们！
t_en: Oh, it's {NICKNAME} and March! I didn't expect you two to be the first ones to show up!

t_ch: 不愧是你们。
t_en: Not bad.

t_ch: 小菜一碟。
t_en: Piece of cake!

t_ch: 哼哼，在本姑娘面前，小小的解谜机关根本不在话下～
t_en: With me here it was a walk in the park!

t_ch: 快把你们的资格徽章交出来！
t_en: Save us all some time and hand over the Mastery Mark!

t_ch: 喂，{NICKNAME}，我们是来挑战冠军候补的，不是来打劫的……
t_en: Uh... {NICKNAME}, we're here to challenge them, not rob them...

t_ch: 不过，这家伙说的也没错啦——我们就是为了徽章来的！
t_en: ...but that is what we're here for! That Mastery Mark is ours!

t_ch: 哼哼哼，哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink, oink! Oink!

t_ch: 哼哼，口气不小嘛……
t_en: Well, well, someone's feeling pretty confident...

t_ch: 那么，是时候让你们领会下「机械热潮」的厉害了！要上了，佩拉！
t_en: Well then, it's time you felt the power of "Mechanical Fever!" Pela, let's show them what we're made of!

t_ch: 区区数值游戏罢了…我和希露瓦会让你们输得心服口服的！
t_en: Just a simple game of numbers... You'll see what we mean!

t_ch: 嗯…你是谁？决胜乐园的挑战者吗？
t_en: Hm, and you are? Victory Zone challengers, I presume?

t_ch: 让我看看…你们还没有点亮这片区域的所有灯炉——那我可不能放你们继续前进了。
t_en: Looks like you haven't bothered to light up all the lamps in this area yet... I can't just let you off the hook.

t_ch: （看来在点亮附近的所有灯炉之前是没办法前进了……）
t_en: (It seems we won't be able to proceed until all the nearby lamps are illuminated...)

t_ch: 门已经开了，你们就继续前进吧——这座决胜乐园里最难的谜题正等着你们呢！
t_en: Fine, fine, the gate's open. Have fun dealing with the toughest challenge in the Victory Zone.

t_ch: 哈哈，真是酣畅淋漓的战斗，好久没有这样乐在其中过了！
t_en: Now that was intense! I can't remember the last time I had so much fun.

t_ch: 没想到…你们的算计，在我之上……
t_en: How did you manage that... I guess you just got lucky this time!

t_ch: 多亏了三月七！
t_en: It was all March!

t_ch: 就是就是，多亏了我！
t_en: Yeah, it was all me!

t_ch: 区区数值游戏罢了！
t_en: Just a simple game of numbers!

t_ch: 你…可恶，好不甘心……
t_en: Ugh... You're so annoying...

t_ch: 现在的我，比你更摇滚！
t_en: Looks like I'm even more rock 'n' roll than you, now!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我承认了，你拥有真正的摇滚之魂！
t_en: Haha, I have to admit, you definitely have that rock 'n' roll flair.

t_ch: 愿赌服输——拿去吧，这就是<color=#dbc291ff>「残响回廊的资格徽章」</color>，恭喜你们！
t_en: Here, take it — <color=#dbc291ff>the Corridor of Fading Echoes Mastery Mark</color> — you've earned it!

t_ch: 另外，这是给你们的礼物：<color=#dbc291ff>「以太拓展芯片」</color>。只要把芯片里的数据注入到以太灵中，就可以让它习得特殊固有能力了。
t_en: I have something else for you also: An <color=#dbc291ff>Aether Expansion Chip</color>. Integrating its data into an Aether Spirit will grant them new, innate abilities.

t_ch: 哼哼，哼哼哼，哼！
t_en: Oink! Grunt! Oink, oink!

t_ch: 哈，小家伙看起来挺开心的——它很信任你们呢。
t_en: Look how happy they are! It's clear they trust you a lot.

t_ch: 「次元扑满」…这可是特别稀有的以太灵。但它刚才在对决中的样子有些奇怪，是出了什么事吗？
t_en: A "Warp Trotter..." That's a pretty rare Aether Spirit... but they were acting kind of strange in the battle — is there something wrong with them?

t_ch: 欸嘿，其实我们也不知道……
t_en: Eh-he, to be honest, we don't actually know...

t_ch: 公司的一小撮员工似乎也想把这个小家伙抢到手。我们是为了保护它才把{TEXTJOIN#54}带在身边的。
t_en: ...but it seemed like some IPC troublemakers were out to get them — that's why {TEXTJOIN#54} is tagging along with us — we're looking after them.

t_ch: 话说回来，希露瓦你似乎比我们更了解以太灵——你能看出这个小家伙出了什么问题吗？
t_en: Serval, you seem to know more about Aether Spirits than us — can you tell us what's up with this little guy?

t_ch: 不好意思，我也不太清楚…公司的科技相当复杂，有些原理我也还没搞清楚。
t_en: Sorry, I'm not quite sure either... IPC tech is pretty complex — there are some principles I'm still trying to wrap my head around.

t_ch: 这样啊…要是能找到托帕小姐问问，说不定还能有些眉目。
t_en: I see... Perhaps if we were able to find Miss Topaz, she'd be able to give us some clues.

t_ch: 哼，哼哼，哼……
t_en: Oink!!! Oink! Oink...

t_ch: 可爱的小家伙…就拜托你们好好照顾它了。带着它在决胜庆典中一路过关斩将，拿下冠军吧。
t_en: They're just too precious... and they're counting on you for protection! Take them with you and snatch the championship!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，谢谢佩拉，我们一定会的。
t_en: Hehe, thanks Pela — we'll do our best!

t_ch: 说到这个——你们听说了吗？那位神秘<color=#dbc291ff>「高手玩家」</color>的故事。
t_en: Speaking of champions — have you heard the story of the mysterious "<color=#dbc291ff>elite player</color>?"

t_ch: 高手玩家？那是谁？
t_en: Elite player? Who?

t_ch: 是啊，那是谁？我们还没听说过呢，这人很厉害吗？
t_en: Yeah, who's that? Are they some sort of gaming prodigy?

t_ch: 我们的名气已经这么大了？
t_en: Has my fame reached such heights already?

t_ch: 哎，你想什么呢。那说的肯定不是咱俩啊……
t_en: Oh, please! It's pretty clear they're not talking about you...

t_ch: 嗯…咳咳。这位玩家，你们应该是认识的——我和希露瓦也认识，但乔瓦尼先生只让我们透露这些信息了。
t_en: Ahem, well... This player isn't a stranger — we know them, too, but Mr. Giovanni has asked us not to divulge too much information.

t_ch: 乔瓦尼？他在搞什么名堂？
t_en: Giovanni? I wonder what he's up to...

t_ch: 这我们就不清楚了。他让我和佩拉点到为止…他说，这样故事才会变得有意思。
t_en: We're not entirely sure... He said something about "adding an element of mystery to the story."

t_ch: 真是个怪人……
t_en: Okay, that's weird...

t_ch: 总之，如果你们的目标也是<color=#dbc291ff>「冠军首席」</color>的话，恐怕免不了与那位玩家一战。
t_en: In any case, if your goal is also to become the <color=#dbc291ff>champions</color>, then it's likely you'll run into them sooner or later.

t_ch: 努力锻炼，好好享受游戏的过程吧——祝你们能拿下那个称号！
t_en: Remember, train hard and relish every moment! The title awaits you!

t_ch: 哼哼！哼！
t_en: Oink, oink, oink!

t_ch: 哈～真是好激烈的对决啊。那些机关谜题也挺好玩，感觉这游戏确实还挺有意思的。
t_en: Ha~ Now that battle was really intense! Those puzzles were such a blast, too — I think I'm hooked!

t_ch: 确实不错。
t_en: I have to agree!

t_ch: 哼哼，那咱们俩想到一块去啦。
t_en: Great minds really do think alike!

t_ch: 真的很好玩吗…
t_en: Huh... Really...?

t_ch: 欸，你不这么觉得吗？我还以为你也乐在其中呢。
t_en: What's the matter? I thought you were having fun.

t_ch: 你要是实在不情愿的话…就当陪陪我，好嘛？这种庆典还挺难得的，我真的想好好体验一下……
t_en: Well, if you're not into it, could you at least keep me company...? Pleeeease! It's not like we get to celebrate these kinds of festivals every day...

t_ch: 我开玩笑的，确实很有意思。
t_en: Just kidding. I'm actually enjoying myself.

t_ch: 那就听你的好了。
t_en: Alright, alright...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thanks {NICKNAME}, you're the best!

t_ch: 让我看看…下一站应该是<color=#dbc291ff>「流云渡的决胜乐园」</color>了。
t_en: Hmm, Let me see... Our next stop should be the <color=#dbc291ff>Cloudford Victory Zone</color>!

t_ch: 感觉今天是来不及了…不如我们就在歌德宾馆休息一晚，明天再乘列车出发吧？
t_en: Buuuut it's already getting late... How about we head back to Goethe Hotel for a bit and then set out on the Express tomorrow?

t_ch: 好不容易回来一趟，我还挺想念他们家那枕头的呢～
t_en: I mean, it's not often we get the chance to stay there — and I really miss their pillows!

t_ch: 没问题，我们走。
t_en: Sounds good to me. Let's go.

t_ch: 好耶！那我们就出发，前往歌德宾馆～！
t_en: Great! Fluffy pillows here we come!

t_ch: 哼哼！哼——
t_en: Oink, oink! Oink...

t_ch: 歌德宾馆，歌德宾馆～我们又回来啦！
t_en: Ahhhh, Goethe Hotel~ Home sweet home!

t_ch: 哼哼！哼哼，哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink! Oink!

t_ch: 咦，{TEXTJOIN#54}？你怎么了？
t_en: Hey, what is it, {TEXTJOIN#54}?

t_ch: 喂，你们俩！对，就是你们俩！
t_en: Hey, it's those two twerps again!

t_ch: 上次就是你们坏了好事！快把那只扑满还给我们！
t_en: It's the two who ruined our plans! Hand over the Trotter, now!

t_ch: 哈？又是你们，自顾自地说什么呢！明明是你们到处抢别人的以太灵！
t_en: Ugh, you two again!? We're minding our own business — you're the ones going around stealing people's Aether Spirits!

t_ch: {TEXTJOIN#54}现在可是我们的伙伴，怎么可能随便交给你们这种人！
t_en: {TEXTJOIN#54} is like family now! Why would we just hand them over to someone like you?

t_ch: 有本事你们就发起对决！
t_en: Let's settle this with a duel, if you've got the guts!

t_ch: 就是就是，有本事你们就跟我们打一场，赢了再说！
t_en: Yeah! If you think you've got what it takes, then bring it on!

t_ch: 就是就是，三月七说得对！
t_en: Yeah, what she said!

t_ch: 有本事你们就跟我们打一场，赢了再说！
t_en: If you think you've got what it takes, then bring it on!

t_ch: 可恶…两个臭小鬼，竟敢看不起我们！这已经是第二次了！
t_en: You brats dare underestimate us? You won't get away with it twice!

t_ch: 第二次了！我要教你们加倍奉还！
t_en: I'll teach you two twerps a lesson you'll never forget!

t_ch: 可恶…竟然又输了……
t_en: Grr... Not again!

t_ch: 又输了…好讨厌的感觉——！
t_en: Another failure... The shame, the shame!

t_ch: 哼哼！哼哼，哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink! Oink!

t_ch: 哎，别跑！我们有话要问！
t_en: Hey, come back! We want to ask you something!

t_ch: …这群人到底怎么回事啊？！一句话也不说就夹着尾巴逃了，刚才不还很神气嘛？
t_en: ...What's with these people!? They talk the talk then run off with their tails between their legs...

t_ch: 而且，{TEXTJOIN#54}到底是怎么惹到他们的……
t_en: ...and what did poor {TEXTJOIN#54} do to end up in their bad books?

t_ch: 哼哼…哼……
t_en: Oink... Oink...

t_ch: 别怕别怕，他们已经走啦。只可惜没从他们身上问出{TEXTJOIN#54}的事……
t_en: Aww, don't worry, it's all right, they're gone now. It's just too bad we couldn't get any clues about our little friend {TEXTJOIN#54}...

t_ch: 哎——真是的！本来心情还好好的，这下让他们给搅浑了……
t_en: Ugh, seriously! I was in such a good mood, too, until they came along and spoiled it...

t_ch: 今晚先好好休息一下。
t_en: Let's get a good night's rest.

t_ch: 不值得跟那种人怄气。
t_en: They're not worth getting angry over.

t_ch: 你说得对，累了一天，我们还是好好休息，不想这些了。
t_en: You're right, we've had a long day. Let's just get some rest and forget about them.

t_ch: 哼哼…哼！哼哼！
t_en: Oink... Oink Oink! Oink!

t_ch: 看，乔瓦尼先生果然也在这里！
t_en: Hey, look, it's Mr. Giovanni!

t_ch: 好久不见啊，{M#哥们}{F#姐们}！
t_en: Long time no see!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这熟悉的感觉——{M#哥们}{F#姐们}说话还是这么有意思！
t_en: Hah, still got the sense of humor, huh?

t_ch: 哎，你要去哪儿呀？照雪灯炉在另一个方向哦。
t_en: Hey, where are you going? The Snowshine Lamps are the other way.

t_ch: 嘿，{NICKNAME}，休息得怎么样？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, sleep well?

t_ch: 哼哼？哼——
t_en: Oink? Oink...

t_ch: 睡得很香。
t_en: Like a log.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我也是。
t_en: Hah, me too.

t_ch: 好累，根本睡不醒…
t_en: Ugh... I'm tired... Please, five more minutes...

t_ch: 哈？但我看你脸色还行呀？别装啦，我看你就是想赖床吧……
t_en: Huh? You don't look that tired to me... Stop playing around.

t_ch: 既然休息好了，那就开始今天的行程吧？
t_en: Since we're all refreshed, ready to head out for the day?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「流云渡的决胜乐园」</color>——我们来啦！
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Cloudford Victory Zone</color> — here we come!

t_ch: 我们到啦！只要从这里乘坐星槎，就可以抵达<color=#dbc291ff>「流云渡的决胜乐园」</color>了。
t_en: We've arrived! From here we need to take a starskiff to the <color=#dbc291ff>Cloudford Victory Zone</color>.

t_ch: 不过，仙舟联盟居然同意了公司的这种请求——感觉真是难以想象。
t_en: It's hard to imagine that the Xianzhou Alliance actually agreed to accommodate the IPC's request, don't you think?

t_ch: 只是租借场地吧。
t_en: They're only leasing the venue.

t_ch: 有道理——仔细想想，好像也不是多大的事。
t_en: Hmm, I guess you're right... maybe it isn't such a big deal.

t_ch: 这就是格局。
t_en: It's about the bigger picture.

t_ch: 确实，怎么说呢…真不愧是那位景元将军。
t_en: Yeah, that makes sense... General Jing Yuan knows how to handle things.

t_ch: 所以，你准备好了吗，{NICKNAME}？我们出发吧～
t_en: So, ready to head out, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 哼哼！哼！
t_en: Oink, Oink, oink!

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 出发——这次也要拿到资格徽章！
t_en: Here we go! We'll have another Mastery Mark in no time!

t_ch: 哈，是{NICKNAME}和三月小姐！没想到能在这里见到二位。
t_en: Aha, it's always a pleasure to bump into you, trailblazers. I didn't expect to see you here.

t_ch: 到目前为止，二位的感觉如何？应该已经熟悉这款游戏了吧？
t_en: I imagine you must be quite familiar with the game by now, how have you been faring?

t_ch: 得心应手！
t_en: Things couldn't be better. We're in our element!

t_ch: 哈哈，不愧是你们！
t_en: Haha, I knew it!

t_ch: 中规中矩。
t_en: We'll figure it all out eventually.

t_ch: 这款游戏是这样的——上手容易精通难哪。
t_en: It's been said that this game is easy to pick up, but difficult to master!

t_ch: 完全没懂…
t_en: Lost... I'm completely lost...

t_ch: 没关系，慢慢来——熟能生巧嘛。
t_en: Not to worry, just take your time... practice makes perfect, after all!

t_ch: 总之，能看到二位还在热情游戏，我也就放心了！
t_en: In any case, seeing both of you still enthusiastic about the game puts my mind at ease!

t_ch: 可惜，我得先失陪了——身为活动赞助人，要忙的事可真不少啊。
t_en: Sadly, I have to bid you farewell. As the event's sponsor, I have a lot on my plate!

t_ch: 辛苦了，乔瓦尼先生。
t_en: You must be pretty tired, Mr. Giovanni.

t_ch: 哼哼…哼！
t_en: Oink... Oink!

t_ch: 不辛苦，应该的。预祝二位顺利拿下<color=#dbc291ff>「流云渡的资格徽章」</color>……
t_en: Oh, it's nothing! I wish you both the best of luck on your quest for the <color=#dbc291ff>Cloudford Mastery Mark</color>.

t_ch: 这里竟然有这么多人！早知道把玉兆带来做直播了——
t_en: Whoa, so many people here! If I'd known, I would've brought my jade abacus with me for a livestream...

t_ch: 哈哈，看来是又来了一位热情的挑战者啊。
t_en: Haha, it looks like we've got another eager challenger!

t_ch: 哎呀呀，这地头可真热闹！各位莫不也是那《以太战线》的玩家，来这流云渡挑战什么冠…冠军候补的？
t_en: This place is buzzing! I take it you're both Aetherium Wars players, here to take on Cloudford's champs— ...uh, championship contenders?

t_ch: 好活泼的女孩子！你看着…不像仙舟本地人呀？
t_en: Someone's full of energy! You... don't look like a Xianzh—

t_ch: 哼哼？哼！哼哼？
t_en: Oink? Oink! Oink, oink?

t_ch: 小姐姐真是火眼金睛！小妹我呀，的确不是仙舟本地人～
t_en: How observant! You're right, I'm not a Xianzhou native~

t_ch: 我本名是「格妮薇儿」，后来呢，一位仙舟朋友根据这本名，又给我起了个地道的仙舟名字——
t_en: My real name is Guinevere, but a Xianzhou friend came up with an authentic Xianzhou name based on the pronunciation.

t_ch: 桂、乃、芬！桂为嘉木，乃者「是」也，芬即芳香，是不是好听又好记？
t_en: Gui, nai, fen! "Gui" means fine wood, "nai" means to exist, and "fen" means fragrant — rolls right off the tongue, doesn't it?

t_ch: 相遇是缘——咱们缘分一场，从今儿起啊，咱几个就都是朋友啦！我平时在那长乐天撂地杂耍，各位有空可来捧个场哈～
t_en: Fate has brought us together — that makes us friends! I often perform at Exalting Sanctum, so if you've ever got time, come and enjoy the show!

t_ch: 你好，桂乃芬。
t_en: Nice to meet you, Guinaifen.

t_ch: 有空一定捧场！
t_en: Deal!

t_ch: 好嘞，就等您这句话呢！嘿嘿。
t_en: I was hoping you'd say that! Hehe.

t_ch: 我叫三月七，{M#他}{F#她}是{NICKNAME}，这边这位是庆典的赞助人——乔瓦尼先生。
t_en: I'm March 7th — this is my fellow trailblazer, {NICKNAME}, and this is Mr. Giovanni — he's sponsoring this event.
